_comment: ServiceNow Flow Designer - VM Provisioning Approval Workflow
_comment2: Import via: Flow Designer → Import → paste this JSON
_comment3: Replace all placeholder sys_ids after import

flow.name: VM Provisioning Approval
flow.description: Approval workflow for RHEL VM provisioning requests from Red Hat Developer Hub. On approval, triggers Ansible Automation Platform to provision the VM.

config.subject: VM Request Received - {{trigger.request_item.vm_name}}
VM Request Approved - Provisioning Started for {{trigger.request_item.vm_name}}

fields.work_notes: VM provisioning request was rejected by approver.

config.subject: VM Request Rejected - {{trigger.request_item.vm_name}}

_manual_import_steps.description: Since Flow Designer JSON import is limited, follow these steps in ServiceNow UI
steps: 1. Go to Flow Designer (search 'Flow Designer' in nav)
4. Add Trigger: Service Catalog → select 'Provision RHEL Virtual Machine' item
5. Add Action: 'Send Notification' → configure 'request received' email to requester
6. Add Action: 'Ask for Approval' → Approvers: Manager of Requested For
7. Add Flow Logic: 'If' → Approval State is 'Approved'
8. In 'If' branch: Add Action → 'REST Step' → Message: 'AAP - Launch VM Provisioning Job' → Method: 'launch'
9. Map variables from catalog item to REST step input vars
11. In 'Else' branch: Update request state + rejected email
